description: Make /status render as a compact transcript card with Web-aligned runtime, session, context, provider quota, warning/error, tool, unavailable, and redaction semantics.

userStories.title: Create shared status fixtures and view-model assertions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic status states so Web and Ink render the same runtime concepts.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared status fixtures cover full, partial, unavailable, zero usage, high usage, warning, error, secret-bearing, non-OpenAI provider, queued, processing, streaming, and disposed states
View-model tests assert title, owner, session, profile, model, runtime, cwd, queue/processing/streaming, context, provider quota, credits, tools, warnings, and errors when present
Tests distinguish missing usage as unavailable and zero usage as 0.0%
Tests assert provider labels and plan/reset/remaining details come from descriptor data and are not OpenAI-hardcoded
Tests assert secrets are redacted before Web or Ink rendering
Focused shared status tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render compact Ink status card with bars and unavailable states
userStories.description: As an SSH operator, I want /status to show runtime health without a verbose debug dump.
acceptanceCriteria: Ink status card primary line summarizes runtime state, session/model, and abbreviated owner without long owner scope dominating the screen

Status card supports session id/title, cwd/workspace, processing, streaming, queued count, disposed/error state, context usage, provider quota, plan, credits, tools, owner/profile/model/runtime, warnings, and errors
Context and provider quota render text bars such as ███░░░ 25.0% when available and meaningful ASCII/text fallback under NO_COLOR or limited glyph conditions
Unavailable context/provider data renders compactly as unavailable, not blank, zero, or multiple verbose debug lines
Warnings/errors use high-signal markers and redact secrets
Narrow-width output keeps session/status/error/quota meaning visible
Focused Ink status render tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Place /status in transcript flow for live controller path
userStories.description: As a TUI user, I want /status output to appear chronologically in the transcript.
acceptanceCriteria: /status appends an execution.command row followed by a tool.status row/card immediately above the prompt/input
/status does not set state.message with the status payload or render a detached header status panel
Running/streaming rows visible before /status remain before the command/status rows
/status with no active session, partial runtime state, or source errors renders a bounded status/error row instead of crashing
Controller tests cover normal, picker-open, no-session, partial-data, source-error, and streaming-tail cases
Focused controller tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keep Web TerminalStatusCard parity hooks and shared descriptor use
userStories.description: As a Web Compact Terminal user, I want the Web status card to remain the reference for status semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Web TerminalStatusCard or equivalent Web Compact Terminal path consumes the shared status descriptor/view model for supported fields
Web output/source exposes stable hooks or test ids for shared card kind, status fields, context usage, provider quota, warnings, errors, and unavailable states where feasible
Web regression tests use the same status fixtures as Ink status tests or derive from the same fixture source
Web visuals are not redesigned except for semantic hooks needed for tests
npm run chat-ui:typecheck passes
npm run chat-ui:build passes
Focused Web status tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate status cards through PTY artifacts
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want terminal evidence that /status looks compact and appears in the right place.
acceptanceCriteria: PTY live/default or closest practical local path runs pibo tui:sessions, executes /status, and asserts command row plus status card appear in transcript flow
PTY deterministic fixture path covers provider quota/context bars when live provider quota is unavailable
PTY artifacts include full or partial status, unavailable status, narrow width, and NO_COLOR=1 where feasible
Artifacts include raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, metadata, input/assertions, exact commands, and visual artifact path or fallback reason
Observed screen does not contain a verbose status dump, dominant long owner id, or header-only status payload
Focused PTY status tests pass
Full npm test passes before this PRD is marked complete
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
